questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A solution of 200 mL of 1 M KOH is added to 200 mL of 1 M HCl and the mixture is well shaken. This rise in temperature <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> is noted. The experiment is repeated by using 100 mL of each solution and increase in temperature <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is again noted. Which of the following is correct?
options: <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> is four times as large as <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>
questions.solution:
Heat produced by 200 Meq. of KOH + HCl
=13.7×2001000kcal
This is used to raise the temperature of 400 mL. Solution say T1
Thus, 13.7×2001000=400×S×T1
Now, heat produced by 100 Meq of KOH + HCl
=13.7×1001000
Thus, -13.7×1001000=200×S×T2
It is evident that T1=T2 .
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The combustion of benzene l gives CO2g and H2Ol. Given that heat of combustion of benzene at constant volume is -3263.9 kJ mol-1 at 25°C; the heat of combustion in kJ mol-1 of benzene at constant pressure will be

R=8.314 JK-1 mol-1
questions.solution:
C6H6l+152O2g→6CO2g+3H2O(I)    ∴  ∆ng= -32

Use, ΔH=ΔU+ΔngRT

=-3263.5×103-32×8.314×298×10-3 

 =-3267.6 kJ mol-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: In an isobaric process, when temperature changes from $T_1$ to $T_2, \Delta S$ is equal to
questions.solution:
The entropy change for a process, when $T$ and $P$ are the variables is given by
$\Delta S=C_P \ln \frac{T_2}{T_1}-R \ln \frac{P_2}{P_1}$
For an isobaric process $P_1=P_2$. Hence the above equation reduces to
$C_P \ln \frac{T_2}{T_1}=\Delta S$
or $\Delta S=2.303 C_P \log \frac{T_2}{T_1}$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For which of the following reactions, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∆</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∆</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">U</mi></math>?
questions.solution:
∆H=∆U+∆ngRT

2HI (g)→H2 (g)+I2 (g)

∆ng=1+1-2=0

so, ∆H=∆U.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A diatomic gas at pressure $P$, compressed adiabatically to half of its volume, what is the final pressure?
questions.solution:
For adiabatic conditions, $P V^\gamma=$ Constant
$P_1 V_1^\gamma=P_2 V_2^\gamma ; V_2=\frac{1}{2} V_1$
$P_2=P_1\left(\frac{V_1}{V_2}\right)^\gamma \quad$ [For diatomic gas, $\gamma=1.4$ ]
$P_2=P_1\left(\frac{V_1 \times 2}{V_1}\right)^{1.4}$
$P_2=P_1(2)^{1.4}=(2)^{1.4} P$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: \(\Delta H_{\text {fusion }}\) of a substance is ' \(x\) ' and \(\Delta H_{\text {vap }}\) is ' \(y\) ', then \(\Delta H_{\text {sublimation }}\) will be
questions.solution:
\(\begin{aligned}
& \text {Solid } \stackrel{\text { fusion }}{\longrightarrow} \text { Liquid }\qquad \Delta H_{\text {fusion }}=x \\
& \text { Liquid } \stackrel{\text { vaporisation }}{\longrightarrow} \text { Gas } \qquad \Delta H_{\text {vap }}=y \\
& \text { Solid } \stackrel{\text { sublimation }}{\longrightarrow} \text { Gas } \qquad \Delta H_{\text {sub }} \\
&
\end{aligned}\)
So, \(\Delta H_{\text {sub }}=\Delta H_{\text {fusion }}+\Delta H_{\text {vap } \cdot}, \Delta H_{\text {sub }}=x+y\).
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Bond enthalpies of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AB</mi></math> are in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If bond enthalpy of formation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AB</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>KJ</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>mol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>.</mo></math> The bond enthalpy of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is
questions.solution:
Assume bond strength of A2=2X, then,

B2=X, AB=2X

ΔrHΘ=∑ΔdissHΘ-(R)∑ΔdissHΘ(P)

12 A2+12 B2→AB

X+X2 - 2X = -100 kJ mol-1X = 200 kJ mol-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: In isolated system, find the condition for spontaneous reaction.
questions.solution:
For an isolated system, $q=0, w=0$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \Delta U=0 \\ & \Delta G=\Delta H-T \Delta S\end{aligned}$
For spontaneous reaction, $\Delta G=-v$ e. $\Delta H=0$ for an isolated system.
Thus, $\Delta S>0$ for the reaction to be spontaneous.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A gas occupies 2 litres at STP. It is provided 58.63 joule heat so that its volume becomes 2.5 litre at 1 atm. Change in its internal energy in joules is
questions.solution:
Work is done by a system irreversibly  (Wsys)=-Pext∆V ⇒(Wsys)=-(1×105)N/m2×(2.5-2)×10-3m3=50
From 1st Law of Thermodynamics: ∆U=wsys+qsys ∆U=-50+58.63J=8.63  J
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: 1 mole of an ideal gas expands isothermally reversibly from 2 litre to 4 litre and 3 moles of the same gas expands from 2 litre to $x$ litre and doing same work, what is ' $x$ '?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { } w=n R T \ln \frac{V_2}{V_1} \\ & R T \ln \frac{4}{2}=3 R T \ln \frac{x}{2} \\ & \ln 2=\ln \left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^3 \\ & x^3=16 \Rightarrow x=(16)^{\frac{1}{3}}=4^{\frac{2}{3}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Given:

Cgraphite+O2g→CO2g ;ΔrHo=−393.5 kJ mol−1

H2g+12O2g→H2Ol;ΔrHo=-285.8 kJ mol-1

CO2g+2H2Ol→CH4g+2O2g;ΔrHo=+890.3 kJ mol-1

Based on the above thermochemical equations, the value of ΔrHo at 298 K for the reaction

Cgraphite+2H2g→CH4g will be:
questions.solution:
The standard enthalpy of formation is defined as the change in enthalpy when one mole of a substance in the standard state (1 atm of pressure and 298.15 K) is formed from its pure elements under the same conditions.

The enthalpy of combustion of a substance is defined as the heat energy given out when one mole of a substance burns completely in oxygen.

The formation reaction of methane is 

Cgraphite+2H2g→CH4g

The heat of combustion data for C(graphite), H2g and CH4g

∆Hfo=∑∆HCombustionreactants-∑∆HCombustionproducts∆HfoCH4=∆HCoCgraphite+2×∆HCoH2g-∆HCoCH4g

∆HfoCH4=-393.5-2×285.8+890.3
ΔfHo of CH4 (g)=−74.8 kJ/mol
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A gas undergoes change from state <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> to state <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>. In this process, the heat absorbed and work done by the gas is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mo> </mo><mtext>and</mtext><mo> </mo><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>, respectively. Now gas is brought back to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> by another process during which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math> of heat is evolved. In this reverse process of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo> </mo><mi>to</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>.
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mo> </mo></math>of the work will be done by the gas
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math> of the work will be done by the surroundings on gas
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math> of the work will be done by the surroundings on gas
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math> of the work will be done by the gas
questions.solution:
for A to Bq=5W= −8 J,ΔUAB=dq+dw=5−8ΔUAB=−3



for B to AΔUBA=dq+dwΔUBA= +33=-3 +WBA       WBA=6 J
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The reversible expansion of an ideal gas under adiabatic and isothermal conditions is shown in the figure. Which of the following statement is incorrect?
questions.solution:
In an isothermal process, temperature is constant

T2=T1

Area under P - V graph is more is isothermal

Wisothermal>wadiabatic

Now ΔUisothermal= 0

In adiabatic T1V1γ-1=T3V3γ-1

T3T1=V1V3γ-1

T3<T1 Hence internal energy decreases

ΔUisothermal>ΔUadiabatic
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The combination of plots which does not represent isothermal expansion of an ideal gas is

(A)

(B)

C

(D)
questions.solution:
PV= constant (isothermal expansion)

P∝1V. The graph will be a straight line, y=mx+c. So, the above graph is correct.



PV= constant (isothermal expansion)

On increasing or decreasing P, PV becomes constant. So, the above graph is correct.

A, C graphs are correct.



PV= constant (isothermal expansion)

P∝1V. The graph will be a straight line, y=mx+c. So, the above graph is incorrect.



ΔU=nCvdT

and for isothermal process, ΔU=0. So, the above graph is incorrect.

Options B, D are incorrect.

questions.q:
The variation of equilibrium constant with temperature is given below :

TemperatureEquilibriumConstantT1=25°CK1=10T2=100°CK2=100

The values of ΔH°, ΔG° at T1 and ΔG° at T2 (in kJ mol-1 ) respectively, are close to [use R=8.314JK-1mol-1]
questions.solution:
T1=323K  T2=373K
k1=10  k2=100
logk2k1=ΔH2.303R1T1-1T2

log10010=ΔH2.303×8.3141298-1373

log10=ΔH2.303×8.31475298×373

ΔH=2.303×8.314×298×37375=28.4KJ

At T1=25°C=298K,K1=10

ΔG=-2.303RT1logk 1

=-2.303×8.314×298×log(10)

=-2.303×8.314×298×1

=-5.7KJ

At T2=100°C=373K  K2=100

ΔG=-2.303RT2logK2

=-2.303×8.314×373×log(10)2

=-2.303×2×8.314×373×1

=-14283.7J

=-14.29KJ

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Data given for the following reaction is as follows :

FeO(s)+C(graphite)⟶Fe(s)+CO(g)

Substance ΔfH°kJmol-1 ΔS°Jmol-1 K-1
FeO(s) -266.3 57.49
C(graphite) 0 5.74
Fe(s) 0 27.28
CO(g) -110.5 197.6

The minimum temperature in K at which the reaction becomes spontaneous is_____.(Integer answer)
questions.solution:
Tmin=Δ°HΔ°S

ΔoHrxn=Δf°H(Fes)+Δf°H(COg)-Δf°H(FeOs)+Δf°H(Cgraphite)

=[0-110.5]-[-266.3+0]

=155.8 kJ/mol

Δ°Srxn=Δ°S(Fes)+Δ°S(COg)-Δ°S(FeOs)+Δ°SC(graphite )

=[27.28+197.6]-[57.49+5.74]

=161.65 J/mol.K

Tmin=155.8×103 J/mol161.65 J/mol-K=963.8 K

≈964 K (nearest integer)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: The relation between pressure P and volume V is given by <math><mtext>P</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>V</mtext></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup></math> = constant. If the percentage decrease in volume is <math><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> , then the approximate percentage increase in pressure is
questions.solution:
P V14=Constant

% decreases in volume =14

dvv×100=14

Now, PV14=a

log⁡P+14log⁡v=log⁡a

dpp+14dvv=0

100×dPP+14dVV×100=0

dPP×100=-14dVV×100

=14×14=116
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
One mole of an ideal monoatomic gas is subjected to changes as shown in the graph. The magnitude of the work done (by the system or on the system) is _______ J (nearest integer)



Given : log 2 = 0.3, ln 10 = 2.3
questions.solution:
1 to 2 , Isobaric process
2 to 3, Isochoric process
3 to 1, Isothermal process

Using work under isobaric, isochoric and isothermal processes respectively:

W = W1→2 + W2→3 + W3→1W = ((-P(V2 - V1) + 0 + (-P1V1(lnV2V1))=((-1(40 - 20) + 0 + (-1 × 20 (ln2040))=620 J
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
